message.content:
Update the Claude Code statusLine configuration to add the following fields to the status line:
1. Context remaining (replace or add alongside current context usage %)
2. Session cost
3. Model in use
4. Agent in use
5. Skill in use

The current status line script is at `/Users/saurabhtyagi/.claude/statusline-command.sh` and settings are at `/Users/saurabhtyagi/.claude/settings.json`. Please read the current script first, then update it to include all these new fields. Use appropriate colors/separators to keep it readable.

content.text: Let me read the current script and settings first.

2	# Claude Code status line - mirrors Powerlevel10k Pure style

6

8	model=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.model.display_name // ""')

14

21	  branch=$(git -C "$cwd" symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null || git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)
22	  dirty=$(git -C "$cwd" status --porcelain 2>/dev/null)
23	  if [ -n "$dirty" ]; then

content.text:
Good. Now I have the full picture. A few notes before updating:

- The JSON input does not include a session cost field, so that field is not available from the status line input. I will omit it and note this.
- "Skill in use" is not a standard JSON field in the status line input either. The closest concept is `output_style.name` (the active output style/skill). I will use that.
- "Agent in use" maps to `agent.name`.
- "Context remaining" will replace the current `ctx:used%` with `ctx:remaining%` using the pre-calculated `remaining_percentage` field.

input.old_string:
#!/bin/sh
# Claude Code status line - mirrors Powerlevel10k Pure style
# Shows: user@host  cwd  git-branch*  model  context%

input=$(cat)

cwd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir // .cwd // ""')
model=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.model.display_name // ""')
used_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.used_percentage // empty')

# user@host in grey (ansi 242)
user=$(whoami)
host=$(hostname -s)

# Shorten home directory to ~
short_cwd=$(echo "$cwd" | sed "s|^$HOME|~|")

# Git branch (skip optional locks)
git_branch=""
if git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  branch=$(git -C "$cwd" symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null || git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)
  dirty=$(git -C "$cwd" status --porcelain 2>/dev/null)
  if [ -n "$dirty" ]; then
    git_branch="${branch}*"
  else
    git_branch="${branch}"
  fi
fi

# Context usage indicator
ctx_part=""
if [ -n "$used_pct" ]; then
  ctx_part=$(printf " ctx:%.0f%%" "$used_pct")
fi

# Build status line with ANSI colours
# grey=\033[38;5;242m  blue=\033[38;5;75m  reset=\033[0m
printf "\033[38;5;242m%s@%s\033[0m  \033[38;5;75m%s\033[0m" "$user" "$host" "$short_cwd"

if [ -n "$git_branch" ]; then
  printf "  \033[38;5;242m%s\033[0m" "$git_branch"
fi

if [ -n "$model" ]; then
  printf "  \033[38;5;242m%s\033[0m" "$model"
fi

if [ -n "$ctx_part" ]; then
  printf "\033[38;5;242m%s\033[0m" "$ctx_part"
fi

printf "\n"
input.new_string:
#!/bin/sh
# Claude Code status line - mirrors Powerlevel10k Pure style
# Shows: user@host  cwd  git-branch*  model  agent  skill  ctx-remaining%

input=$(cat)

cwd=$(echo "$input"          | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir // .cwd // ""')
model=$(echo "$input"        | jq -r '.model.display_name // ""')
remaining_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.remaining_percentage // empty')
agent_name=$(echo "$input"   | jq -r '.agent.name // empty')
skill_name=$(echo "$input"   | jq -r '.output_style.name // empty')

# user@host in grey (ansi 242)
user=$(whoami)
host=$(hostname -s)

# Shorten home directory to ~
short_cwd=$(echo "$cwd" | sed "s|^$HOME|~|")

# Git branch (skip optional locks)
git_branch=""
if git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  branch=$(git -C "$cwd" symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null || git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)
  dirty=$(git -C "$cwd" status --porcelain 2>/dev/null)
  if [ -n "$dirty" ]; then
    git_branch="${branch}*"
  else
    git_branch="${branch}"
  fi
fi

# Context remaining indicator — shown in amber when low (<= 20%), green otherwise
ctx_part=""
if [ -n "$remaining_pct" ]; then
  remaining_int=$(printf "%.0f" "$remaining_pct")
  if [ "$remaining_int" -le 20 ]; then
    ctx_color="\033[38;5;214m"   # amber/orange — low context warning
  else
    ctx_color="\033[38;5;71m"    # muted green — healthy context
  fi
  ctx_part=" ctx:${remaining_int}%"
fi

# Colour palette
grey="\033[38;5;242m"
blue="\033[38;5;75m"
teal="\033[38;5;73m"    # agent name
purple="\033[38;5;141m" # skill/output-style name
reset="\033[0m"

# --- Build the line ---

# user@host  cwd
printf "${grey}%s@%s${reset}  ${blue}%s${reset}" "$user" "$host" "$short_cwd"

# git branch
if [ -n "$git_branch" ]; then
  printf "  ${grey}%s${reset}" "$git_branch"
fi

# model
if [ -n "$model" ]; then
  printf "  ${grey}%s${reset}" "$model"
fi

# agent (only when present — i.e. Claude was started with --agent)
if [ -n "$agent_name" ]; then
  printf "  ${teal}agent:%s${reset}" "$agent_name"
fi

# skill / output style (suppressed when value is "default" to reduce noise)
if [ -n "$skill_name" ] && [ "$skill_name" != "default" ]; then
  printf "  ${purple}skill:%s${reset}" "$skill_name"
fi

# context remaining
if [ -n "$ctx_part" ]; then
  printf "  ${ctx_color}%s${reset}" "$ctx_part"
fi

printf "\n"

content.content: The file /Users/saurabhtyagi/.claude/statusline-command.sh has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

content.text:
The script is updated. The settings.json already points to that script and does not need changes.

Here is a summary of every change made and a note on what could not be fulfilled.

---

Fields added / changed

| Field | Source in JSON | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Context remaining | `.context_window.remaining_percentage` | Replaces the old "used %" display. Shown in muted green normally; switches to amber when remaining drops to 20% or below. |
| Model in use | `.model.display_name` | Was already present; kept as-is. |
| Agent in use | `.agent.name` | Only rendered when Claude was launched with `--agent`. Hidden otherwise so the line stays clean. |
| Skill in use | `.output_style.name` | Maps to the active output style (e.g. "Explanatory", "Learning"). Hidden when the value is "default" to avoid visual noise. |

Fields that cannot be added

- **Session cost** — the status line JSON input does not expose cost or token-pricing data. There is no field to read this from, so it cannot be included. You would need an external script that reads the transcript file and calculates cost independently; that is a separate, more complex integration.

---

The updated script is at `/Users/saurabhtyagi/.claude/statusline-command.sh`.

Note for future changes: this status line was configured by the `statusline-setup` sub-agent. If you want to make further changes to the status line, ask Claude to update the status line and it will invoke that same agent to handle the changes correctly.
